description: Processes new client intake form submissions by checking for duplicate emails in the CRM, updating existing records or creating new ones, and notifying the Slack team of new leads.

trigger.description: Receives form submission payloads from Typeform via webhook. Protected by HMAC-SHA256 secret token validated in step_1. Response mode set to 'Last Node' so the final Respond to Webhook node controls the HTTP response.

steps.name: Validate Webhook Signature
steps.description: Validates the Typeform-Signature HMAC-SHA256 header using the raw request body bytes (not re-serialized JSON) to ensure the request is authentic before any processing. Rejects unauthorized requests. The webhook trigger must have 'Raw Body' enabled so $input.first().json.rawBody contains the original request bytes as a string.
steps.error_handling: Throw on invalid signature; n8n will halt execution. Webhook response mode is 'Last Node'; on error n8n returns 500.

steps.name: Extract and Normalize Form Fields
steps.description: Extracts and normalizes key fields from the Typeform webhook payload into a flat structure for downstream use. Uses optional chaining to avoid runtime errors when fields are missing.
steps.error_handling: Stop workflow on code error; missing optional fields default to empty string

steps.name: Validate Required Fields
steps.description: Validates that required fields (email, first_name, last_name) are present and non-empty, outputting a boolean flag for the gate.

steps.name: Check Fields Valid
steps.description: Routes to CRM lookup if fields are valid, otherwise stops processing. Compares the boolean fields_valid flag using boolean equals operator.
steps.error_handling: Route to fail branch on invalid fields

steps.name: Look Up Email in CRM
steps.description: Queries the internal CRM API to check if a contact with the submitted email already exists. Uses queryParameters for safe URL encoding. Authenticated via CRM API key credential.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 3 times; stop workflow and alert on persistent failure

steps.name: Merge CRM Response with Form Data
steps.description: Merges the CRM lookup response with the original form data and determines whether this is a new or existing contact. Defensively handles multiple possible CRM response shapes (contacts array, data array, or direct array).

steps.name: Check If Existing Contact
steps.description: Routes to update existing record if contact exists, or create new record if contact is new. Compares the boolean is_existing_contact flag using boolean equals operator.
steps.error_handling: Default to new contact path on ambiguous state

steps.name: Update Existing CRM Contact
steps.description: Updates the existing CRM contact record with the latest form submission data. Authenticated via CRM API key credential.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 3 times; stop workflow and alert on persistent failure. Handle 409 conflict by treating as existing record already up to date.

steps.name: Set Updated Record Result
steps.description: Sets a normalized result object for the updated contact indicating the record was updated (not new), so no Slack notification is sent.

steps.name: Create New CRM Contact
steps.description: Creates a new CRM contact record for the new client submission. Authenticated via CRM API key credential. On 409 conflict (race condition duplicate), treats the contact as already existing.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 3 times; stop workflow and alert on persistent failure. On 409/422 conflict response, treat as existing record (race condition guard).

steps.name: Set New Lead Result
steps.description: Sets a normalized result object for the newly created contact, flagging it as a new lead for Slack notification.

steps.name: Check If New Lead for Slack
steps.description: Routes to Slack notification only if the contact is a newly created lead. Both step_9 (updated) and step_11 (created) connect here directly, eliminating the need for the previously broken merge node. Compares boolean is_new_lead flag using boolean equals operator.
steps.error_handling: Skip Slack on error to avoid duplicate notifications

steps.name: Send New Lead Slack Notification
steps.description: Posts a Slack message to the leads channel with the new client's details using the native n8n Slack node for managed authentication.

parameters.text:
={{ ':tada: New client intake received!
*Name:* ' + $json.first_name + ' ' + $json.last_name + '
*Email:* ' + $json.email + '
*Phone:* ' + ($json.phone || 'N/A') + '
*Company:* ' + ($json.company || 'N/A') + '
*Submitted At:* ' + $json.submitted_at + '
*CRM ID:* ' + $json.contact_id }}

steps.error_handling: Retry up to 3 times; log failure but do not stop workflow

steps.name: Build Final Webhook Response
steps.description: Constructs the final JSON response payload confirming success. Receives input from both the new-lead path (after Slack, step_13) and the existing-contact path (step_12 false branch). Feeds into the explicit Respond to Webhook node.
steps.error_handling: Return generic success if fields unavailable

steps.name: Respond to Webhook
steps.description: Explicitly returns the final JSON response to the Typeform webhook caller with HTTP 200, using the payload built in step_14. This ensures a clean, controlled HTTP response regardless of which branch was taken.
steps.error_handling: Return HTTP 500 on node error; n8n default error handler applies

gates.notes: Fail branch stops processing for invalid submissions; no downstream step needed
True = existing contact -> update (step_8); False = new contact -> create (step_10)
Updated contact result flows directly into Slack gate (step_12). Merge node removed to fix passThrough input1-only bug.
New lead result flows directly into Slack gate (step_12). Both step_9 and step_11 connect here, replacing the broken merge node.
True = new lead -> notify Slack (step_13) then build response (step_14); False = existing -> build response (step_14) directly
After Slack notification, build final response payload
Feed built response into explicit Respond to Webhook node for clean HTTP response

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Return HTTP 500 with error message; log to n8n execution log for developer review

output.destination: Webhook response, CRM contact record, Slack #new-leads channel
output.format: JSON response to caller via Respond to Webhook node; CRM record created/updated; Slack message posted for new leads
output.description: Each intake submission results in an upserted CRM record and, for new contacts, a Slack notification with full client details

credentials_needed: CRM API key (HTTP header auth - configured as n8n httpHeaderAuth credential on step_5, step_8, step_10)
Slack Bot Token (managed via n8n Slack credential on step_13)
Typeform Webhook Secret (stored as n8n environment variable TYPEFORM_WEBHOOK_SECRET, used in step_1 with raw body HMAC-SHA256)
security.pii_handling: Email, name, and phone are processed in memory only; stored in CRM; not logged in n8n execution data
security.data_flow: Typeform -> n8n Webhook (HMAC-SHA256 signature validated in step_1 using raw body bytes) -> CRM API (authenticated) -> Slack API (authenticated). All communication over HTTPS. Webhook raw body is preserved before JSON parsing to ensure HMAC validation correctness.
security.race_condition_note: Concurrent duplicate submissions are partially mitigated by handling 409/422 conflict responses from the CRM CREATE endpoint. For stronger guarantees, a CRM-side unique constraint on email is recommended.

cost_estimate: Negligible per run - all calls are to internal or free-tier APIs; Slack API is free; n8n self-hosted has no per-execution cost

expected.validation_error: Missing or invalid required fields (email, first_name, last_name)

test_cases.name: New client with optional fields missing (no phone or company)

expected.error: Unauthorized: Invalid or missing Typeform webhook signature

test_cases.name: Malformed payload - answers array missing entirely

expected.validation_error: Missing or invalid required fields (email, first_name, last_name)

test_cases.name: Email with special characters (plus-addressing) - URL encoding test

expected.record_action: created or updated depending on CRM state

test_cases.name: Race condition - CRM returns 409 conflict on create (concurrent duplicate)
CRM returns non-standard response shape (data array instead of contacts array)
